{"step_by_step_thinking": "Option A states that the enzyme attaching an amino acid to tRNA is aminoacyl-tRNA synthetase, which is true according to the documents. Option B mentions that although composed of a single strand of RNA, each molecule contains several short, double-helical regions, which is also supported by the documents. Option C claims that the amino acid attachment is always to the 3' end of the molecule, but the documents indicate that the amino acid is attached to the 3' acceptor site, not necessarily the 3' end of the entire molecule. Option D states that there is at least one tRNA for each of the 20 amino acids, which is accurate. Therefore, the false statement is C.", "answer_choice": "C"}
